metadata.name: Preflight Check
metadata.description: Validates all preconditions before a workflow step is allowed to execute. Acts as a safety gate that catches configuration errors, missing files, unresolved variables, and broken skill references before they cause runtime failures mid-step.

triggers.trigger: Automatically before every workflow step execution.
Manually via `preflight` command in the Director.

outputs.output: Resolved variable map for the step.

check.name: Variable Resolution
check.description: Scan the entire step definition for `{variable}` placeholders and verify that every single one resolves to a concrete value from the workflow context, environment, or config chain.

procedure.step: Parse the step definition and extract all `{variable}` patterns from every string field recursively.
Build a unique set of all variable names referenced by the step.

For each variable, attempt resolution in this order:
Variables defined in the step's own `locals` block.
Variables set by previous steps in the workflow context.
Parameters passed to the workflow at invocation time.
Variables from the current episode's configuration.
Variables from the project-level COOKIE configuration.
Default values defined in the COOKIE framework itself.
Record the resolved value and its source for each variable.

If ANY variable cannot be resolved from any source: - Mark this check as FAILED. - Collect all unresolved variables into an error report. - Trigger `on_error.unresolved_variable` from error-recovery.xml. - HALT: The step must not execute.
If all variables resolve, store the complete resolved variable map in the preflight report for use during step execution.

on_failure.report: List all unresolved variables with: - Variable name - Location in step definition where it appears - Sources that were checked - Suggestion for where to define the missing variable

check.name: File Existence Validation
check.description: After all variables are resolved, verify that every file path referenced by the step points to an actual file (or directory) on disk. This prevents runtime errors from missing assets.

scan_targets.target: All resolved output directory paths (parent directories must exist)
Config file paths referenced in the step

procedure.step: Collect all file and directory paths from the step definition after variable resolution has been applied.

Categorize each path:
Files the step reads. Must exist. Failure is critical.
Files the step may read if available. Marked with `optional: true`. Missing is acceptable — logged as info.
Parent directories for step outputs. Must exist or be creatable.
Files referenced for metadata or validation only. Missing triggers a warning but does not block execution.

For each path, check existence on disk: - For files: verify the file exists and is readable. - For directories: verify the directory exists and is listable. - For output directories: verify the parent exists; create if missing and the step has `create_output_dirs: true`.

Compile the results: - If any required_input is missing → FAIL with `missing_file` error. - If any output_directory parent is missing and cannot be created → FAIL. - If optional_inputs are missing → log INFO, continue. - If references are missing → log WARNING, continue.

on_failure.action: Halt workflow for critical failures; warn for non-critical.
on_failure.report: For each missing file: - Resolved absolute path - Category (required/optional/output/reference) - The input declaration that referenced it - Suggested fix (check path, run setup, create directory)

check.name: Skill Loadability Validation
check.description: Verify that every skill referenced by the step exists and can be loaded. Skills are defined by SKILL.md files in the skills directory. A skill is considered loadable if its SKILL.md file exists and contains valid skill metadata.

procedure.step: Extract all skill references from the step definition. Skills are referenced by name in `skill:` fields or in step type declarations (e.g., `type: skill/generate-image`).
For each skill reference, resolve the skill directory path: `{project-root}/_cookie/skills/{skill-name}/SKILL.md`
Verify the SKILL.md file exists at the resolved path.

Parse the SKILL.md file header to validate it contains the required metadata fields:
The skill's display name. What the skill does. Declared input parameters. Declared output parameters.
Verify that the step's input parameters match the skill's declared input schema. Flag type mismatches or missing required inputs as warnings.
If the SKILL.md file is missing or unparseable: - Mark this check as FAILED. - Report which skill could not be loaded and why. - Suggest running skill discovery or checking the skill name.

on_failure.action: Halt workflow. A missing skill cannot be executed.
on_failure.report: For each unloadable skill: - Skill name as referenced in the step - Expected SKILL.md path - Reason for failure (file missing, parse error, missing metadata) - Available skills that are similar (fuzzy name match)

check.name: Config Inheritance Chain Validation
check.description: The COOKIE framework uses a layered configuration system where project config inherits from framework defaults, and episode config overrides project config. This check verifies the entire chain resolves without conflicts or missing links.

layer.name: framework_defaults
layer.description: Base defaults shipped with the COOKIE framework.

layer.name: project_config
layer.description: Project-level overrides set by the creator.

layer.name: step_overrides
layer.path: Inline in the workflow step definition.

procedure.step: Identify which config keys the current step depends on. This includes any config references in the step definition (e.g., `config.resolution`, `config.model`, `config.cost_limit`).
For each config key, walk the inheritance chain from highest priority (step_overrides) to lowest (framework_defaults) to find the effective value.

Verify that required config layers exist on disk: - framework_defaults MUST exist. If missing → FAIL. - project_config MUST exist. If missing → FAIL. - episode_config is optional. If missing → use project_config values.

Check for config conflicts: - Type mismatches between layers (e.g., string vs number). - Deprecated keys that have been renamed in newer versions. - Unknown keys that do not exist in framework_defaults (potential typos).
Produce the merged configuration for the step with full traceability (which layer each value came from).

on_failure.action: For missing required config files: HALT. For conflicts and warnings: LOG and continue with best-effort resolution.
on_failure.report: - Config key in question - Value found at each layer - Conflict description (if any) - Effective resolved value - Source layer for the resolved value

preflight_report.description: After all checks complete, produce a consolidated preflight report. This report is stored in the workflow context and optionally displayed to the user depending on verbosity settings.

format.field: ISO 8601 timestamp of when preflight ran.

Array of individual check results:
ID of the check (e.g., resolve_variables). PASS, FAIL, or WARN. critical or warning. Array of detailed findings. How long the check took to run.

display_rules.rule: Show all results including PASS details.
Show all results plus resolution chain traces.

dry_run.description: When the workflow is run in dry-run mode, the preflight check executes for ALL steps in the workflow (not just the next step). This provides a comprehensive validation report before any execution begins.

procedure.step: Iterate through every step in the workflow definition.

Run all four preflight checks for each step. Note: some variables may not be resolvable in dry-run mode because they depend on outputs from previous steps. These are flagged as DEFERRED rather than FAILED.
Produce a consolidated dry-run report covering all steps.
Present the report to the user with a summary: - Total steps validated - Steps that passed all checks - Steps with warnings - Steps with failures - Steps with deferred checks
